description: Records a MuppetMasher X question about why EksAyn Anderson, a Utah author and self-described behavioral expert, was in UVU front-row reserved seating. Framed as a preserved question, not an accusation, with attached images and benign possible explanations.

title: Nick — UVU Witness
description: Documents UVU witness "Nick" (per MuppetMasher) describing a "loud pop," a group at the top reportedly cheering when Kirk was hit, and a right-side neck trajectory, cross-referenced against ballistics and shooting-location analyses.

title: Shot in the Chest, Then the Neck — One Witness, Two Accounts
description: Documents a witness compilation (per CharlotteLee_88) where one witness describes a chest/heart wound then a neck wound on another station an hour later, while another witness describes the neck. Presented as an open question, not an accusation.

title: Cooper Brown — Man Who Stopped a Witness on Camera
description: Documents a video (per DiligentDenizen) identifying Cooper Brown as the man who stopped a young TPUSA person from giving on-camera witness testimony while covering his ears, and a same-day Fox News segment calling him an event volunteer 15 feet away. Notes the contested, shifting identification; presented with attribution, not as an accusation.

title: Witnesses
description: Index of firsthand UVU witness accounts, linking individual witness pages and featuring the chest-then-neck clip. Covers recurring patterns: gunshot sound profiles, crowd reactions, and trajectory descriptions versus the official single-rifle narrative.

title: Shot to the Heart — The Repeated Theme and the Shaped-Charge Question
description: Collects early on-camera witnesses repeatedly describing a "shot to the heart," including retired officer Angie Martin's account and a second TV-news clip (@charlie_ward77), documents the shaped-charge theory and the coordinated-narrative question. Presented as open questions, not conclusions.

title: Wrong Witnesses — Why the Same Wrong Detail?
